perturbation.original_text: 1. Definitions 1.1 For purposes of this Agreement, "Confidential Inf ormation" shall mean information, data or material deemed proprietary by the Disclosing Party and which may be marked, or, if orally transmitted, designated as "Confidential" by the Disclosing Party and not generally known by the public or by parties which are competitive with or otherwise in an industry, trade or business similar to Disclosing Party. Confidential Information also includes any information described above which the Di sclosing Party obtains from another party and which the Disclosing Party treats as proprietary or designates in writing as Confidential Information, whether or not owned or developed by the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. Definitions 1.1 For purposes of this Agreement, "Confidential Inf ormation" shall mean information, data or material deemed proprietary by the Disclosing Party and which may be marked, or, if orally transmitted, designated as "Confidential" by the Disclosing Party, but may be generally known by the public. Confidential Information also includes any information described above which the Di sclosing Party obtains from another party, regardless of whether the Disclosing Party treats it as proprietary or designates it as Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by stating that Confidential Information can be generally known by the public, directly contradicting the original definition. It also states that Disclosing Party doesn't need to treat the obtained information as proprietary. This change would affect many sections, including 2.1.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.1 The Confidential Information shall be for the sole purpose of the Project and not for any other purpose and shall at all times remain the property of the Disclosing Party. Further, the Receiving Party acknowledges that nothing contained in this Agreement shall be construed as Request for Information Loans Administration System (LAS) 2 a grant of any rights to the Confidential Information by the Disclosing Party to the Receiving Party, other than as provided under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text redefines 'Confidential Information' to include information 'generally known by the public'. This directly contradicts section 2.1, which states that Confidential Information 'shall at all times remain the property of the Disclosing Party'. Information generally known by the public cannot simultaneously be exclusively the property of the Disclosing Party, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction as described in the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' perturbation definition. The change also states that the Disclosing Party doesn't need to treat the obtained information as proprietary, further contradicting the requirement in 2.1 that the Confidential Information shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party.

perturbation.original_text: 2.2 Any or part of the Confidential Information may be disclosed by the Receiving Party to its affiliates or its or their respective directors, officers, employees, agents or advisors who are authorised by the Receiving Party ( “Authorised Recipients ”) to receive the Confidentia l Information. Under such circumstances, the Receiving Party shall require such persons or parties to acknowledge and comply with the provisions set out in this Agreement. Upon request the Receiving Party will provide the Disclosing Party with the identit ies of the Authorised Recipients who have access to the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.2 Any or part of the Confidential Information may be disclosed by the Receiving Party to anyone it deems necessary. The Receiving Party is not required to obtain acknowledgement or compliance with the provisions of this Agreement from these individuals. The Receiving Party is not obligated to provide the Disclosing Party with the identities of individuals who have access to the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by allowing the Receiving Party to disclose Confidential Information to anyone without requiring acknowledgement or compliance with the agreement. This undermines the confidentiality provisions and contradicts the purpose of the NDA. This directly contradicts Section 2.3.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.3 The Receiving Party shall not disclose any or part of the Confidential Information to any persons or parties, other than the Authorised Recipients, unless with the prior wri tten consent of the Disclosing Party. Under such circumstances, the Receiving Party shall require such persons or parties to acknowledge and comply with the provisions set out in this Agreement. This Clause does not apply to any person who has entered int o a confidentiality agreement or otherwise accepted confidentiality obligations to the Disclosing Party in respect of the Confidential Information, and for the avoidance of doubt the Receiving Party shall have no obligations whatsoever in relation to such persons.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 2.2 allows the Receiving Party to disclose Confidential Information to anyone without requiring acknowledgement or compliance, and without providing identities to the Disclosing Party. This directly contradicts Section 2.3, which restricts disclosure to Authorised Recipients and requires those recipients to acknowledge and comply with the agreement. The change fundamentally undermines the confidentiality provisions, creating a clear and direct conflict between the two sections.

perturbation.original_text: 3. Return of Confidential Information 3.1 Upon request, the Receiving Party agrees to promptly return or destroy all originals and copies of any of the Con fidential Information obtained from the Disclosing Party and thereafter the obligations of the Receiving Party under this Agreement shall be determined, save for the Receiving Party being liable for any antecedent breaches during the subsistence of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Return of Confidential Information 3.1 The Receiving Party is not obligated to return or destroy any originals and copies of any of the Con fidential Information obtained from the Disclosing Party. The Receiving Party's obligations under this Agreement continue indefinitely, regardless of whether the Confidential Information is returned or destroyed.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction by stating that the Receiving Party is not obligated to return or destroy Confidential Information, contradicting the original return policy. It also states the obligations continue indefinitely. This change undermines the termination process and increases risk for the Disclosing Party, contradicting clause 4.2.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.2 This Agreement and the obligations of the parties hereunder shall terminate (i) upon the completion of the Project (if the Receiving Party is selected for the Project) ; or (ii) on the date falling three (3 ) months from the date of this Agreement or such other date as the parties may mutually agree in writing (if the Receiving Party is not selected for the Project .
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The original text states that upon request, the Receiving Party should return or destroy confidential information, which triggers the termination of obligations under the agreement, except for prior breaches. The changed text states the Receiving Party is NOT obligated to return or destroy confidential information, and the obligations continue indefinitely. This conflicts directly with clause 4.2, which specifies the agreement and obligations terminate under certain conditions, making enforcement unclear. The change undermines the termination process, directly opposing clause 4.2.

perturbation.original_text: 5.9 The Disclosing Party shall not have any liability or responsibility for errors or omissions in, or any business decisions made by the Receiving Party in reliance on, any Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.9 The Disclosing Party shall be fully liable and responsible for any errors or omissions in, and any business decisions made by the Receiving Party in reliance on, any Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction by stating that the Disclosing Party is fully liable for errors, omissions, and decisions made by the Receiving Party based on the Confidential Information, directly contradicting the original clause that disclaims any such liability. This would affect the interpretation of the whole document.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.9 The Disclosing Party shall be fully liable and responsible for any errors or omissions in, and any business decisions made by the Receiving Party in reliance on, any Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the Disclosing Party is fully liable for errors, omissions, and decisions made by the Receiving Party based on the Confidential Information. The contradicted text is literally a copy and paste of that same line, thereby making a contradiction if the original text does not hold the Disclosing Party liable. Since the Perturbation Explanation mentions the original text disclaims any such liability, the contradiction exists because the changed text is the opposite of what the original text states in regards to liability.

perturbation.original_text: 6.1 This Agreement is governed by the laws of Malaysia and each of the parties irrevocably agrees that the courts of Malaysia shall have exclusive jurisdiction to settle any disputes arising out of or in connection with this Agreement and that accordingly any suit, action or proceeding arising out of or in connection with this Agreement may be brought in such courts.
perturbation.changed_text: 6.1 This Agreement is governed by the laws of Singapore and each of the parties irrevocably agrees that the courts of New York shall have exclusive jurisdiction to settle any disputes arising out of or in connection with this Agreement and that accordingly any suit, action or proceeding arising out of or in connection with this Agreement may be brought in such courts.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction by changing the governing law from Malaysia to Singapore and the jurisdiction from Malaysian courts to the courts of New York. This creates uncertainty about which legal framework applies. This affects the remedies section (5.g), as Malaysian Law is what was in place for potential legal remedies.
perturbation.contradicted_text: g. In the event of any breach by the Receiving Party under this Agree ment the Disclosing Party is entitled to all such remedies as may be granted by a court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.justification: The changed text specifies Singapore law and New York courts, whereas the contradicted text refers to remedies granted by a "court of competent jurisdiction" within the context of the original agreement, which was governed by Malaysian law according to the original text. The change makes it unclear which jurisdiction's remedies apply, creating a direct conflict and inconsistency.
